title: 02 · Combine
text:
Output B — output/pigmentation_gwas_harmonized.csv: the catalog pull (Output A) plus every per-source file in output/sources/, all on the shared schema, unioned and deduplicated by rsID. This step is a pure union/dedup — every source is already mapped onto the common columns in its own workbook, so nothing source-specific happens here except choosing which source wins a shared rsID.
Set CATALOG_VERSION to a filename under output/catalog/versions/ to pin a specific catalog pull; the default "latest" uses the current Output A.


Code
import sys, glob
sys.path.insert(0, "scripts")
import pandas as pd
from harmonize import to_harmonized, const, combine, HARMONIZED_COLS

CATALOG_VERSION = "latest"   # or e.g. "pigmentation_gwas_catalog_20260701T002827Z.csv"

cat_path = ("output/catalog/pigmentation_gwas_catalog.csv" if CATALOG_VERSION == "latest"
            else f"output/catalog/versions/{CATALOG_VERSION}")
cat_raw = pd.read_csv(cat_path, dtype=str, keep_default_na=False)

title: 02 · Combine
text:
Output B — output/pigmentation_gwas_harmonized.csv: the catalog pull (Output A) plus every per-source file in output/sources/, all on the shared schema, unioned and deduplicated by rsID. This step is a pure union/dedup — every source is already mapped onto the common columns in its own workbook, so nothing source-specific happens here except choosing which source wins a shared rsID.
Set CATALOG_VERSION to a filename under output/catalog/versions/ to pin a specific catalog pull; the default "latest" uses the current Output A.


Code
import sys, glob
sys.path.insert(0, "scripts")
import pandas as pd
from harmonize import to_harmonized, const, combine, HARMONIZED_COLS

CATALOG_VERSION = "latest"   # or e.g. "pigmentation_gwas_catalog_20260701T002827Z.csv"

cat_path = ("output/catalog/pigmentation_gwas_catalog.csv" if CATALOG_VERSION == "latest"
            else f"output/catalog/versions/{CATALOG_VERSION}")
cat_raw = pd.read_csv(cat_path, dtype=str, keep_default_na=False)

title: 02 · Combine
section: Map the catalog onto the shared schema
text:
Map the catalog onto the shared schema
The catalog’s or_beta column mixes odds ratios and linear betas without a clean unit, and the v1 pull did not capture the discovery-sample ancestry or the numeric CI. So catalog rows carry effect_type = "unknown" and needs_sumstats = True — an explicit flag that these loci need full summary statistics (or a widened pull) before they are scoring-ready, not a silent gap. effect_size still carries the reported or_beta and eaf the risk-allele frequency, so nothing is thrown away.


Code
cat_map = {
    "rsid":            "rsid",
    "chr":             "chr",
    "pos":             "pos_hg38",
    "build":           const("hg38"),
    "effect_allele":   "risk_allele",
    "eaf":             "risk_freq",
    "effect_type":     const("unknown"),
    "effect_size":     "or_beta",
    "pvalue":          "pvalue",
    "trait":           "mapped_trait",
    "mapped_gene":     "mapped_gene",
    "pubmed":          "pubmed",
    "study_accession": "study_accession",
}
cat_ver = cat_raw["queried_utc"].iloc[0] if "queried_utc" in cat_raw and len(cat_raw) else "unknown"
catalog = to_harmonized(cat_raw, cat_map, source="gwas_catalog",
                        source_version=cat_ver, needs_sumstats=True)
print(f"catalog: {len(catalog)} loci (queried {cat_ver})")


catalog: 440 loci (queried 2026-07-01T00:28:27Z)

title: 02 · Combine
text:
Union all sources and deduplicate
Precedence prefers African-ancestry paper effect sizes over the catalog for shared rsIDs (mirroring the source-of-truth choice the SAPPHIRE pipeline made). A shared rsID keeps the higher-precedence row and its source is rewritten to the +-joined set of contributing sources (e.g. crawford2017+gwas_catalog), so multi-source provenance survives the dedup.


Code
frames = [catalog]
for f in sorted(glob.glob("output/sources/*_harmonized.csv")):
    frames.append(pd.read_csv(f, dtype=str, keep_default_na=False)[HARMONIZED_COLS])

PRECEDENCE = ["crawford2017", "gwas_catalog"]   # earlier = wins a shared rsID
compendium = combine(frames, precedence=PRECEDENCE)
compendium.to_csv("output/pigmentation_gwas_harmonized.csv", index=False)
print(f"{len(compendium)} unique loci -> output/pigmentation_gwas_harmonized.csv")


492 unique loci -> output/pigmentation_gwas_harmonized.csv

Summary


Code
print("rows per source tag:")
print(compendium["source"].value_counts().to_string())
print(f"\nloci flagged needs_sumstats: {(compendium['needs_sumstats'].astype(str) == 'True').sum()}"
      f" / {len(compendium)}")
compendium.head(15)

title: 01 · Catalog pull
text: Output A — output/catalog/pigmentation_gwas_catalog.csv: every pigmentation association the GWAS Catalog files under a small, reviewed set of root traits (and their child traits). Each pull is stamped with the UTC time it hit the catalog and a dated copy is archived under output/catalog/versions/, so you can always go back to exactly what a previous pull returned.

title: 01 · Catalog pull
text: Output A — output/catalog/pigmentation_gwas_catalog.csv: every pigmentation association the GWAS Catalog files under a small, reviewed set of root traits (and their child traits). Each pull is stamped with the UTC time it hit the catalog and a dated copy is archived under output/catalog/versions/, so you can always go back to exactly what a previous pull returned.

title: 01 · Catalog pull
text:
How the pull works
The engine (scripts/gwas_catalog.py) is config-driven: scripts/traits_pigmentation.json names a handful of ontology root traits (EFO/OBA/MONDO short forms) — skin pigmentation, facial pigmentation, freckles, sunburn, suntan, sun sensitivity, melanin measurement — and the catalog’s download endpoint returns every locus filed under each root and its child traits (includeChildTraits=true). That means a study added after this list was authored is still captured, without hardcoding a gene list that would go stale and hide its provenance.
Three fail-loud guarantees protect the pull: every root must return >0 rows; the config’s anchor SNPs (SLC24A5 rs1426654, HERC2 rs12913832) must survive or the run errors; and any mapped trait not in the reviewed snapshot is flagged for review.


Code
import sys, json
sys.path.insert(0, "scripts")
from gwas_catalog import load_config, run_axis, write_cache, assert_anchors

# Cache-first: with a cache present this loads Output A with NO network call, so the
# workbook renders identically every time. Set REFRESH = True to pull fresh from the
# live catalog (which archives a new dated version under output/catalog/versions/).
REFRESH = False

cfg = load_config("scripts/traits_pigmentation.json")
loci, meta = run_axis(cfg, refresh=REFRESH)
if REFRESH:
    write_cache(loci, meta, cfg)

print(f"{len(loci)} lead SNPs — queried {meta.get('queried_utc')}")


  using cached pull from 2026-07-01T00:28:27Z (440 loci) — output/catalog/pigmentation_gwas_catalog.csv
440 lead SNPs — queried 2026-07-01T00:28:27Z

title: 01 · Catalog pull
text:
Anchor check
If either anchor is missing, the pull is not capturing the loci this axis is built on and this cell raises (do not trust the output downstream).


Code
assert_anchors(cfg.axis, loci, cfg.anchors)


  ✓ skin anchors present: SLC24A5 rs1426654, HERC2 rs12913832

title: pigment-gwas
text:
A small atlas of skin-pigmentation GWAS loci, built to seed improved polygenic scores and HIrisPlex-style comparisons. Two outputs, produced by two kinds of workbook:

1 · Catalog pull → output/catalog/pigmentation_gwas_catalog.csv — the latest NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog pull for pigmentation, versioned (every pull archived under output/catalog/versions/).
Per-source workbooks (e.g. Crawford 2017) → one standardized file each in output/sources/.
2 · Combine → output/pigmentation_gwas_harmonized.csv — the catalog plus every per-source file, unioned and deduplicated onto one shared schema.

Adding a source = add one per-source workbook that writes output/sources/<name>_harmonized.csv; the combine step picks it up automatically. See the README for the schema and the add-a-source recipe.

title: Source · Crawford et al. 2017
text:
Crawford et al. 2017 is the defining GWAS of skin pigmentation in African populations (SLC24A5, MFSD12, DDB1/TMEM138, OCA2/HERC2, and others). It is not in the GWAS Catalog download, so the catalog pull in 01_catalog_pull.qmd can never recover it — it has to be added as its own source. This workbook maps Crawford’s Table 1 onto the shared schema and writes one standardized file; the combine step then folds it in.
This is the template for adding any non-catalog source: copy this workbook, point it at a new source table, adjust the mapping, and write output/sources/<name>_harmonized.csv. No existing workbook changes.

title: Source · Crawford et al. 2017
text:
Crawford et al. 2017 is the defining GWAS of skin pigmentation in African populations (SLC24A5, MFSD12, DDB1/TMEM138, OCA2/HERC2, and others). It is not in the GWAS Catalog download, so the catalog pull in 01_catalog_pull.qmd can never recover it — it has to be added as its own source. This workbook maps Crawford’s Table 1 onto the shared schema and writes one standardized file; the combine step then folds it in.
This is the template for adding any non-catalog source: copy this workbook, point it at a new source table, adjust the mapping, and write output/sources/<name>_harmonized.csv. No existing workbook changes.

title: Source · Crawford et al. 2017
section: Source quirks to be honest about
text:
Source quirks to be honest about

Build is hg19 (pos_hg19), whereas the catalog is hg38 — harmonization keeps build per row so nothing silently mis-aligns; rsID is the safe join key.
Effect allele = the derived allele. Crawford codes the whole table against one allele/beta convention, and the sign needs calibrating against a known locus (rs1426654-A is light-associated). We keep the reported beta as effect_size with effect_type = beta; the sign convention is a documented review item for scoring, not a per-locus summary-stats gap, so needs_sumstats = False.
Ancestry is recorded as African (Crawford’s discovery cohorts).



Code
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "scripts")
import pandas as pd
from harmonize import to_harmonized, const

cw = pd.read_csv("data/sources/crawford2017_table1.tsv", sep="\t",
                 dtype=str, keep_default_na=False)

mapping = {
    "rsid":          "rsid",
    "chr":           "chr",
    "pos":           "pos_hg19",
    "build":         const("hg19"),
    "effect_allele": "derived",      # Crawford's effect is coded on the derived allele
    "other_allele":  "ancestral",
    "effect_type":   const("beta"),
    "effect_size":   "beta",
    "pvalue":        "pvalue",
    "trait":         const("skin pigmentation (Crawford 2017 Table 1)"),
    "ancestry":      const("African (Crawford et al. 2017 discovery cohorts)"),
    "mapped_gene":   "nearest_gene",
    "pubmed":        const("29025994"),
}

craw = to_harmonized(cw, mapping, source="crawford2017",
                     source_version="2017_Table1", needs_sumstats=False)
craw.to_csv("output/sources/crawford2017_harmonized.csv", index=False)
print(f"{len(craw)} Crawford loci -> output/sources/crawford2017_harmonized.csv")
craw.head(10)


56 Crawford loci -> output/sources/crawford2017_harmonized.csv
